skill.name: interactive-planner
skill.description: Gather project requirements through structured interactive questions, multi-step scoping, and clear specification synthesis.
skill.summary: Turn vague project ideas into scoped requirements through guided interactive questions.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, browser credential access, network reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick execution patterns. Manual review found these are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, planning labels, OAuth and JWT option text, cookie session examples, and Graphviz diagram labels; no executable code, shell invocation, credential access, network behavior, or prompt-injection attempt was found.

content.value_statement: Unclear project requests often lead to missing requirements and rework. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through structured question batches that produce a clearer specification.
actual_capabilities: Designs batches of up to four interactive questions for project scoping.
Separates planning topics across functionality, architecture, user experience, quality, and constraints.
Uses multi-select options when choices are not mutually exclusive.
Identifies missing information, conflicts, and follow-up questions.
Synthesizes collected answers into an actionable requirement specification.
limitations: It does not implement the scoped project or modify files by itself.
It depends on the host assistant having an interactive question capability.
It can over-question simple tasks if used without judgment.
It cannot verify external technical constraints without separate research.

use_cases.title: Scope a New Product Feature
use_cases.description: Convert a broad feature idea into clear decisions about users, workflows, data, and quality expectations.

use_cases.title: Prepare Engineering Work
use_cases.description: Gather architecture, testing, integration, and deployment requirements before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Clarify Client Requests
use_cases.description: Use structured questions to turn vague client goals into a shared specification for approval.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner scoping for a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use interactive-planner to scope this feature. Ask one short batch of questions, then summarize the requirement and any missing information.

prompt_templates.title: Scope a New Application
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard planning for a new build
prompt_templates.prompt: Use interactive-planner for a new application idea. Ask structured batches about users, core features, technical stack, data, testing, and deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Partial Specification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use interactive-planner to review this partial specification. Ask only about unclear decisions, conflicts, dependencies, and acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Run Deep Requirements Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Use interactive-planner with deep planning. Ask multiple focused question batches, track assumptions, identify unresolved risks, and produce an implementation-ready specification.

output_examples.input: Plan a checkout flow for an e-commerce app.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks about payment methods, account requirements, shipping rules, fraud checks, analytics, and rollout expectations. It then summarizes the selected scope and open risks.

output_examples.input: Clarify requirements for adding real-time collaboration.
output: Question batches cover collaboration roles, document locking, presence indicators, conflict handling, offline behavior, and test coverage.
The final output is a concise specification with decisions, assumptions, and unresolved items.

output_examples.input: Review a partial authentication rewrite plan.
output_examples.output: The assistant focuses on missing choices such as session storage, OAuth providers, migration strategy, security expectations, and rollout sequencing.

best_practices: Start with broad scoping questions before asking technical details.
Use multi-select questions only when several answers can be valid together.
End each planning session with a concise specification and a list of unresolved questions.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for a simple one-file change with clear requirements.
Do not ask overlapping questions that force the same decision twice.
Do not treat selected options as final if the user adds important context in free text.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an assistant gather project requirements through structured interactive questions and then summarize the answers into a specification.

faq.question: Does it write code?
faq.answer: No. It supports planning and scoping before implementation.

faq.question: Why does it use question batches?
faq.answer: Small batches reduce user fatigue and keep each decision focused.

faq.question: Can it handle complex projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends multiple batches for complex work and tracks missing information.

faq.question: Does it require Claude Code?
faq.answer: It is designed around Claude Code style interactive questions, but the planning approach can guide Claude and Codex workflows.

faq.question: When should I avoid it?
faq.answer: Avoid it when the task already has a complete specification or needs immediate implementation.
